zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst於1447年出生在英國肯特書爾斯特時，他的父親William Sidney IV31歲，母親Thomasine Barrington32歲。他於1475年與Lady Anne Brandon在英國英格蘭肯特書爾斯特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1512年在英國英格蘭肯特坦布利其 威爾斯享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst于1447年出生在英国肯特書爾斯特时，他的父亲William Sidney IV31岁，母亲Thomasine Barrington32岁。他于1475年与Lady Anne Brandon在英国英格兰肯特書爾斯特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1512年在英国英格兰肯特坦布利其 威爾斯享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Sydney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): variant of   Sidney  .

variants.name: Sidney
meanings: habitational name from Sidney Farm in Alfold (Surrey), from Old English sīd ‘large, spacious, long’ (dative sīdan) + ēg ‘island’.
possibly also a habitational name from any of the places called Saint-Denis in Normandy, named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Dionysius (see   Dennis  ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst was born in 1447, in Penshurst, Kent, England, his father, William Sidney IV, was 31 and his mother, Thomasine Barrington, was 32. He married Lady Anne Brandon in 1475, in Penshurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1512, in Tunbridge Wells, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst est né en 1447, à Penshurst, Kent, Angleterre, son père, William Sidney IV, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Thomasine Barrington, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Lady Anne Brandon en 1475, à Penshurst, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1512, à Tunbridge Wells, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurstが1447年にイギリスケントペンシュルストで生まれた時，父William Sidney IVは31歳，母Thomasine Barringtonは32歳であった。1475年にイギリスイギリスケントペンシュルストでLady Anne Brandonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1512年にイギリスイギリスケントトゥンブリッジ ウエルズで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst(이)가 1447년 영국 켄트 펜슈르스트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Sidney IV(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Thomasine Barrington(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1475년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 펜슈르스트에서 Lady Anne Brandon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1512년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 툰브리지 웰스에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst родился в 1447 г. в местности пеншурст, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени William Sidney IV было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Thomasine Barrington, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Anne Brandon в 1475 г. в местности пеншурст, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1512 г. в местности тунбридж веллс, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет.
